title: Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
subhed: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.

Days after heatwave, French government cuts climate change adaptation funds
subhed: The Green Fund, which helps local authorities finance environmental protection and climate adaptation projects, is facing a €162.5 million cut, a nearly 20% reduction.

title: Zelensky's rehabilitation of controversial Ukrainian nationalists sparks anger in Israel and Poland
subhed: By attending a reburial ceremony for Andriy Melnyk, a pro-independence leader who collaborated with Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union, Volodymyr Zelensky has backed the country's evolving memorial politics, which have been focused on opposition to Moscow since the February 2022 invasion.

title: The past, present and future cinema of director Céline Sciamma
subhed: From June 3 to 16, the MK2 Bibliothèque movie theater in Paris is hosting the first-ever retrospective dedicated to the filmmaker, featuring all her films, a masterclass and a conversation with actor Adèle Haenel.

title: Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
subhed: Building a more equitable world while respecting planetary limits is possible, economists from the World Inequality Lab assert in a report published Thursday. To achieve this, they say, targeted reductions in consumption in specific sectors, a drastic cut in fossil fuel use and changes in dietary habits will be necessary.

subhed: Before the fragile 70-meter-long embroidery is transported to the United Kingdom in July, a new risk assessment study released on Wednesday has given the move the green light.

title: Who is rich in France?
subhed: Is a childless couple wealthy if they earn €6,438 per month after taxes? A new report seeks to address the lack of set criteria for measuring this demographic.

title: The 20 French cities where the country's wealthiest live
subhed: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.
